summary: This notebook provides a baseline solution for an essay generation competition by leveraging the Gemma 2 2B instruction-tuned model. It demonstrates a complete inference pipeline that loads the model via PyTorch, applies a custom chat template, and generates text conditioned on competition topics. The author loops through the test dataset, generates short essays with a fixed output length, and formats the results for submission. Readers learn how to quickly deploy a pre-trained open-weight LLM locally on Kaggle GPUs without relying on external APIs or complex training setups. The key takeaway is that a straightforward inference loop with a well-formatted prompt can serve as a functional starting point for text generation tasks.
competition_flow: Loads the Gemma 2 2B model, reads competition test topics via pandas, generates essays using a custom prompt template, and saves the outputs to a CSV submission file.
data_reading: Loaded via pandas `read_csv` from Kaggle datasets (`test.csv` and `sample_submission.csv`).
insights: Pre-trained open-weight LLMs can be deployed locally on Kaggle GPUs for rapid baseline generation without API costs.
Custom chat templates are required to properly format prompts for Gemma models.
Adjusting the `output_len` parameter directly controls the length of generated essays.
